Do not use Hungarian notation or type prefixes (e.g., strName, intCount, objCustomer).
Do not use Option Strict Off or Option Explicit Off as it hides type conversion errors.
Do not use Variant-style Object typing for general-purpose variables; always use the strongest applicable type.
Do not use single-character variable names except for simple loop counters (i, j, k).
Do not declare multiple variables on a single line (e.g., Dim a, b, c As Integer).
Do not use module-level or global variables when local scope is sufficient.
Do not shadow variables from outer scopes with identically named inner variables.
Do not use magic numbers or magic strings directly in code; extract them into named constants or configuration.
Do not leave variables uninitialized when a sensible default exists.
